message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in StreamListener

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (debug flag, guarded DEBUG logs at start/end/emit/flush/reset); only touching __init__ and internal methods, no signature/behavior changes to public API besides new debug/debug_logger kwargs.

type: INTENT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Adding idle_timeout_s param, self._last_chunk_ts tracking in __init__, timestamp refresh in receive() on non-empty chunk, and new tick(now_ts=None) method for idle-timeout heartbeat; will reset state unconditionally on timeout. Purely additive - should not conflict with your debug logging as long as we both append new lines rather than rewrite shared blocks.

type: INTENT
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Adding debug/debug_logger params to __init__ (appended after allow_reuse param), self.debug/self._logger init at end of __init__, guarded DEBUG log calls inserted inline in receive() (start-detect ~line 132-140, rolling-end-check ~line 161-166, emit-chunk ~line 168, reset ~line 87-93) and in flush() before each return, plus a small _truncate_preview helper method. Will re-read file before finalizing patch to merge with your idle_timeout changes; let's each keep edits as small localized insertions to minimize conflict.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: Done: added idle_timeout_s param as the new last param in __init__ signature (after allow_reuse), self.idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts init at end of __init__, timestamp refresh added in receive() right after the chunk_message-is-None check (~line 100-103), and a new tick() method inserted between receive() and flush(). Please append your debug/debug_logger params after idle_timeout_s (not after allow_reuse) to avoid a signature merge conflict, and re-read the file now before inserting your log lines since exact line numbers shifted by ~30 lines.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
summary: My sandbox copy of the file doesn't show your idle_timeout_s edits (each of us seems to be working on an isolated copy of the repo, not a shared file). I've completed my debug-logging feature (debug/debug_logger params appended after allow_reuse, guarded DEBUG logs at start-detect/rolling-end-check/emit-chunk/flush/reset) and am finalizing my own patch.txt now; not merging your changes in since they aren't present in my working tree.
